    24		"structural_validation_failed": true,
    25	}
    26	
    27	// ShouldEscalate reports whether status warrants escalating to the next tier.
    28	// Structural failures (e.g. validation errors) do not escalate because a
    29	// smarter model cannot fix a malformed prompt or corrupted bead state.
    30	func ShouldEscalate(status string) bool {
    31		return EscalatableStatuses[status]
    32	}
    33	
    34	// ProviderCooldownDuration is how long an unhealthy harness is skipped before
    35	// re-probing. Five minutes gives most transient errors time to resolve without
    36	// locking out a provider for too long.
    37	//
    38	// Per-harness cooldown tracking moved to the upstream agent service in v0.8.0:
    39	// callers record failures via svc.RecordRouteAttempt and read cooldown state
    40	// via svc.RouteStatus's RouteCandidateStatus.Healthy (ddx-7bc0c8d5).
    41	const ProviderCooldownDuration = 5 * time.Minute
    42	
    43	// FormatTierAttemptBody formats the body of a tier-attempt bead event.
    44	func FormatTierAttemptBody(tier, harness, model, probeResult, detail string) string {
    45		body := fmt.Sprintf("tier=%s harness=%s model=%s", tier, harness, model)
    46		if probeResult != "" {
    47			body += " probe=" + probeResult
    48		}
    49		if detail != "" {
    50			body += "\n" + detail
    51		}
    52		return body
    53	}
    54	
    55	// EscalationWinningExhausted is the sentinel value written into the
    56	// winning_tier field of an escalation-summary body when the escalation loop
    57	// ran through every eligible tier without producing a successful attempt.
    58	const EscalationWinningExhausted = "exhausted"
    59	
    60	// TierAttemptRecord is one row of an escalation trace. It captures the
    61	// tier/harness/model that was tried, the status that attempt returned, and
    62	// the cost/duration the harness reported for that attempt. Skipped tiers
    63	// (no viable provider) are recorded with zero cost and zero duration.
    64	type TierAttemptRecord struct {
    65		Tier       string  `json:"tier"`
    66		Harness    string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
    67		Model      string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
    68		Status     string  `json:"status"`
    69		CostUSD    float64 `json:"cost_usd"`
    70		DurationMS int64   `json:"duration_ms"`
    71	}
    72	
    73	// EscalationSummary is the structured body of a kind:escalation-summary bead
    74	// event. It captures the entire escalation trace so an operator can diagnose
    75	// which tiers were tried, which one won (if any), and how much the path cost.
    76	type EscalationSummary struct {
    77		TiersAttempted []TierAttemptRecord `json:"tiers_attempted"`
    78		WinningTier    string              `json:"winning_tier"`
    79		TotalCostUSD   float64             `json:"total_cost_usd"`
    80		WastedCostUSD  float64             `json:"wasted_cost_usd"`
    81	}
    82	
    83	// BuildEscalationSummary computes the summary body from the ordered list of
    84	// attempts. winningTier is the string of the tier whose attempt succeeded;
    85	// pass "" when the escalation was exhausted, in which case winning_tier is
    86	// set to EscalationWinningExhausted. Total cost is the sum of all attempt
    87	// costs; wasted cost is the sum of attempts whose status is not
    88	// SuccessStatus.
    89	func BuildEscalationSummary(attempts []TierAttemptRecord, winningTier string) EscalationSummary {
    90		out := EscalationSummary{
    91			TiersAttempted: append([]TierAttemptRecord{}, attempts...),
    92			WinningTier:    winningTier,
    93		}
    94		if out.WinningTier == "" {
    95			out.WinningTier = EscalationWinningExhausted
    96		}
    97		for _, a := range attempts {
    98			out.TotalCostUSD += a.CostUSD
    99			if a.Status != SuccessStatus {
   100				out.WastedCostUSD += a.CostUSD
   101			}
   102		}
   103		return out
   104	}
   105	
   106	// BeadEventAppender records append-only evidence events on a bead. Mirrors
   107	// agent.BeadEventAppender so escalation can append events without importing
   108	// agent. *bead.Store satisfies both interfaces.
   109	type BeadEventAppender interface {
   110		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
   111	}
   112	
   113	// AppendEscalationSummaryEvent writes a kind:escalation-summary event to the
   114	// bead with a JSON body describing the tier escalation trace. It is a
   115	// best-effort operation — errors from the underlying store are returned so
   116	// callers can log them, but callers typically ignore the error so telemetry
   117	// failures never abort the main execute-bead flow.
   118	func AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(appender BeadEventAppender, beadID, actor string, attempts []TierAttemptRecord, winningTier string, createdAt time.Time) error {
   119		if appender == nil || beadID == "" {
   120			return nil
   121		}
   122		summary := BuildEscalationSummary(attempts, winningTier)
   123		body, err := json.Marshal(summary)
   124		if err != nil {
   125			return err
   126		}
   127		return appender.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
   128			Kind:      "escalation-summary",
   129			Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("winning_tier=%s attempts=%d total_cost_usd=%.4f wasted_cost_usd=%.4f", summary.WinningTier, len(attempts), summary.TotalCostUSD, summary.WastedCostUSD),
   130			Body:      string(body),
   131			Actor:     actor,
   132			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
   133			CreatedAt: createdAt,
   134		})
   135	}

     2	
     3	import (
     4		"fmt"
     5		"strings"
     6		"sync"
     7	)
     8	
     9	// InfrastructureFailurePatterns are detail substrings that indicate a
    10	// transient infrastructure-level failure (provider 502, network unreachable,
    11	// command not found, auth/quota exhausted) rather than a model-capability
    12	// failure (test failed, build failed, no changes after attempt).
    13	//
    14	// Infrastructure failures should not consume escalation budget — the bead
    15	// should be deferred with a retry-after, not retried at the next tier with
    16	// a more expensive model. The model wasn't given a fair chance.
    17	//
    18	// Patterns are matched case-insensitively as substrings of the failure
    19	// detail string. Any new pattern added here is automatically picked up by
    20	// IsInfrastructureFailure callers.
    21	var InfrastructureFailurePatterns = []string{
    22		// HTTP-level provider unavailability
    23		"502", "503", "504",
    24		"bad gateway",
    25		"service unavailable",
    26		"gateway timeout",
    27		// Network-level unreachability
    28		"connection refused",
    29		"no such host",
    30		"no route to host",
    31		"network is unreachable",
    32		"i/o timeout",
    33		"context deadline exceeded",
    34		// Auth/quota exhaustion (operator-fixable, not a model fault)
    35		"401", "429",
    36		"unauthorized",
    37		"rate limit",
    38		"ratelimit",
    39		"quota exceeded",
    40		"insufficient quota",
    41		"insufficient_quota",
    42		// Subprocess harness binary missing or unstartable
    43		"command not found",
    44		"executable file not found",
    45		"no such file or directory",
    46	}
    47	
    48	// IsInfrastructureFailure reports whether the given failure status + detail
    49	// indicates a transient infrastructure problem the model could not have
    50	// fixed. Only execution_failed can be infrastructure; other escalatable
    51	// statuses are semantic outcomes that should proceed through the tier ladder.
    52	// Returns false for statuses whose detail does not match any known
    53	// infrastructure pattern.
    54	//
    55	// Used by the execute-loop to decide whether to (a) defer the bead with a
    56	// retry-after and try the same tier later, or (b) burn through to the next
    57	// tier as the standard escalation policy.
    58	func IsInfrastructureFailure(status, detail string) bool {
    59		if status != "execution_failed" {
    60			return false
    61		}
    62		if detail == "" {
    63			return false
    64		}
    65		lower := strings.ToLower(detail)
    66		for _, p := range InfrastructureFailurePatterns {
    67			if strings.Contains(lower, p) {
    68				return true
    69			}
    70		}
    71		return false
    72	}
    73	
    74	// DefaultMaxCostUSD is the per-execute-loop dollar cap on accumulated billed
    75	// spend. Exceeding the cap halts further bead claiming so an execute-loop
    76	// run cannot silently burn through arbitrary openrouter / pay-per-token
    77	// credits. Local LLMs and subscription-bundled providers do not count
    78	// toward the cap (see CountsTowardCostCap).
    79	//
    80	// $100 is large enough not to interrupt normal subscription-driven work
    81	// (where billed cost stays at $0) and small enough to surface a clear stop
    82	// signal before runaway openrouter usage.
    83	const DefaultMaxCostUSD = 100.0
    84	
    85	// CostClassBilled is the HarnessInfo.CostClass value the agent uses for
    86	// pay-per-token providers. Free local providers report "free" and
    87	// subscription-bundled providers report "subscription".
    88	const CostClassBilled = "expensive"
    89	
    90	// CountsTowardCostCap returns true when an attempt's reported CostUSD
    91	// should accumulate against the loop's MaxCostUSD. Callers pass the
    92	// HarnessInfo of the harness that ran the attempt; when info is nil the
    93	// safe default is to count (treat unknown as billed).
    94	func CountsTowardCostCap(isLocal, isSubscription bool, costClass string) bool {
    95		if isLocal {
    96			return false
    97		}
    98		if isSubscription {
    99			return false
   100		}
   101		// CostClass "free" or "subscription" never bill; only "expensive" does.
   102		if costClass != "" && costClass != CostClassBilled {
   103			return false
   104		}
   105		return true
   106	}
   107	
   108	// HarnessBilledLookup reports whether a harness's reported CostUSD should
   109	// be accumulated against a CostCapTracker. Implementations typically
   110	// consult a service.ListHarnesses snapshot. A nil lookup is treated as
   111	// "count by default" (the safe option for unknown harnesses).
   112	type HarnessBilledLookup func(harnessName string) bool
   113	
   114	// CostCapTracker accumulates billed cost for a single execute-loop run
   115	// and reports when accumulated spend has reached the configured cap.
   116	// Safe for concurrent use across goroutines (workers may call Add and
   117	// Tripped concurrently).
   118	//
   119	// MaxUSD == 0 disables the cap; Tripped always returns (_, false). The
   120	// Lookup callback decides which harnesses contribute to the running
   121	// total — local and subscription-bundled providers should be excluded
   122	// (see CountsTowardCostCap).
   123	type CostCapTracker struct {
   124		MaxUSD float64
   125		Lookup HarnessBilledLookup
   126	
   127		mu    sync.Mutex
   128		spent float64
   129		// cache memoizes Lookup results so we do not rebuild a service for
   130		// every reported attempt. Protected by mu.
   131		cache map[string]bool
   132	}
   133	
   134	// NewCostCapTracker constructs a tracker with the given dollar cap and
   135	// lookup. Pass maxUSD <= 0 to disable the cap. A nil lookup is treated
   136	// as "count by default" — every harness contributes.
   137	func NewCostCapTracker(maxUSD float64, lookup HarnessBilledLookup) *CostCapTracker {
   138		return &CostCapTracker{
   139			MaxUSD: maxUSD,
   140			Lookup: lookup,
   141			cache:  map[string]bool{},
   142		}
   143	}
   144	
   145	// counts reports whether harnessName's CostUSD should accumulate. The
   146	// result is memoized; callers that want fresh resolution should
   147	// construct a new tracker. Lookups for empty harness names default to
   148	// true (count) so accidentally-missing harness metadata never silently
   149	// bypasses the cap.
   150	func (t *CostCapTracker) counts(harnessName string) bool {
   151		if harnessName == "" {
   152			return true
   153		}
   154		t.mu.Lock()
   155		if v, ok := t.cache[harnessName]; ok {
   156			t.mu.Unlock()
   157			return v
   158		}
   159		t.mu.Unlock()
   160		result := true
   161		if t.Lookup != nil {
   162			result = t.Lookup(harnessName)
   163		}
   164		t.mu.Lock()
   165		t.cache[harnessName] = result
   166		t.mu.Unlock()
   167		return result
   168	}
   169	
   170	// Add accumulates costUSD against the running total when harnessName's
   171	// billing class counts toward the cap. Non-positive cost is ignored.
   172	func (t *CostCapTracker) Add(harnessName string, costUSD float64) {
   173		if costUSD <= 0 {
   174			return
   175		}
   176		if !t.counts(harnessName) {
   177			return
   178		}
   179		t.mu.Lock()
   180		t.spent += costUSD
   181		t.mu.Unlock()
   182	}
   183	
   184	// Spent returns the current accumulated billed total.
   185	func (t *CostCapTracker) Spent() float64 {
   186		t.mu.Lock()
   187		defer t.mu.Unlock()
   188		return t.spent
   189	}
   190	
   191	// Tripped reports whether the accumulated spend has reached MaxUSD. When
   192	// MaxUSD <= 0 the cap is disabled and Tripped returns (_, false).
   193	// Returns the formatted operator-facing detail string when tripped so
   194	// callers can populate a stop-the-loop ExecuteBeadReport.
   195	func (t *CostCapTracker) Tripped() (string, bool) {
   196		if t.MaxUSD <= 0 {
   197			return "", false
   198		}
   199		t.mu.Lock()
   200		spent := t.spent
   201		t.mu.Unlock()
   202		if spent < t.MaxUSD {
   203			return "", false
   204		}
   205		return fmt.Sprintf("cost cap reached: $%.2f billed >= $%.2f cap; raise the cap or set 0 to disable. Subscription and local providers do not count.", spent, t.MaxUSD), true
   206	}
